title: Understanding the Problem
text: To find the frictional torque, we need to understand how the flywheel's rotational motion is being stopped by friction over a given time.

title: Convert Units
text: First, convert the initial angular velocity from revolutions per minute to radians per second. Remember, $$1$$ revolution is $$2\pi$$ radians.

title: Calculate Initial Angular Velocity
text: Calculate the initial angular velocity in radians per second.

title: Angular Deceleration
text: The flywheel comes to rest, meaning its final angular velocity is $$0$$. Use the time given to find the angular deceleration.

title: Calculate Angular Deceleration
text: Use the formula for angular deceleration: \alpha $$=$$ (ω_final - ω_initial) / time.

title: Torque and Moment of Inertia
text: The frictional torque can be found using the relationship between torque, moment of inertia, and angular acceleration: τ $$=$$ I * \alpha.

title: Calculate Frictional Torque
text: Use the moment of inertia (I $$=$$ $$100$$ kg*m**2) and the angular deceleration to find the frictional torque.
